# SESSION BRIEF · BU-trace — Materialised traceability matrix

_Brief version: 1.0 · Author: Paul + Claude · Date: 2026-04-26_
_Priority: Phase 0 chore (deferred from D038's original Phase 0
deliverables list). Lower urgency than features, higher value than
dependency upgrades for "demo something with confidence."_
_Pairs with: D038 (traceability discipline ADR), F06 rule 1 +
F13 + F14 (the building blocks that this script consumes)._

---

## Objective

Build the long-promised `scripts/trace.ts` from D038 §6. Walks the
codebase, scenarios, ADRs, and Build Units; produces a
**materialised traceability matrix** mapping each artefact to its
peers in both directions.

The script lets a developer ask:

- "Which code files back Scenario 20?"
- "Which scenarios does the file `components/CommentList.tsx`
  serve?"
- "Which ADRs constrain BU-comments?"
- "Which scenarios have zero code references?"

…and get answers in under a second, from one command.

Success: `npm run trace SCN-20` prints the full forward + reverse
dependency tree. `npm run trace:check` exits non-zero if any
scenario has zero referencing code (CI-runnable). The matrix is
also written to a markdown file for human reading.

---

## Scope

### Build in this session

**Decision record (recommended, not strictly required):**

- `docs/architecture/decision-log.md` — append `D053 · trace.ts
  output format + check thresholds` (MODIFY, append-only)

**Script:**

- `scripts/trace.ts` (new) — TypeScript, runs via `tsx`. Consumes
  the codebase + scenarios + decision-log. Emits a matrix.
- `scripts/trace.README.md` (new)

**Package wiring:**

- `package.json` (MODIFY — add scripts, do NOT modify existing
  scripts)
  - `"trace": "tsx scripts/trace.ts"`
  - `"trace:check": "tsx scripts/trace.ts --check"`
  - `"trace:matrix": "tsx scripts/trace.ts --matrix"`

**Documentation:**

- `docs/process/traceability.md` (new) — the discipline doc.
  Documents how `@build-unit`, `@spec`, `@scenarios`, ADR refs,
  and testid area prefixes interlock into the matrix the script
  produces. Companion to D038.

**Output artefact:**

- `docs/architecture/traceability-matrix.md` (new — generated by
  `npm run trace:matrix`, committed for browseability). Auto-
  refreshable; CI can fail if drifted.

**CI integration:**

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (MODIFY — add a `Traceability check`
  step running `npm run trace:check` after lint, before tests)

**Tests:**

- `tests/unit/trace.test.ts` (new) — covers the parsing functions
  (extract `@build-unit`, `@spec`, `@scenarios` tags from a file),
  the cross-reference builder, and the check-mode exit codes.
  6+ cases.

### Do NOT touch

- Any feature code in `app/`, `components/`, `server/`, `prisma/`,
  `shared/` — this BU adds tooling, not features
- The eight existing F-rules — no new rules, just consumption of
  the existing tags
- `docs/architecture/decision-log.md` D038 — preserved as the
  philosophical parent; this BU delivers on its §6 commitment but
  doesn't supersede it
- `docs/product/scenarios.md` — read-only input
- Existing `@build-unit` / `@spec` / `@scenarios` tags throughout
  the codebase — read-only input
- Existing seed data, migrations, schema — none of these are
  inputs

### Out of scope for this session

- **Auto-fixing missing references.** The script reports gaps; it
  doesn't insert tags. A human decides whether the gap is
  intentional (parked scenario) or a real omission.
- **Reverse-lookup from a testid.** The matrix scopes to
  `@spec` references in code headers. Testid-level granularity is
  a future enhancement (testids already cluster by area prefix
  per F14, which gives most of the same value).
- **IDE integration / hover-traceability.** Out — would need a
  language-server extension. The CLI is enough for MVP.
- **Real-time watching.** No file-watch mode. Run on demand or in
  CI.
- **Cross-repo traceability.** Single-repo only. If we ever split
  the codebase, the script gets extended.
- **Visual graph output.** Markdown matrix is enough. Mermaid /
  Graphviz output is parking-lot.
- **Coverage of analytics events.** `analytics-events.md` is
  separate from scenarios; the script can pick events up later if
  D038 §5 conventions are tightened.
- **PR-comment integration.** "On every PR, post the diff in the
  traceability matrix" is parking-lot.
- **History / blame mode.** "Who added the SCN-N reference and
  when?" is `git log` territory.

---

## Contracts

### Inputs consumed

The script reads (read-only):

- `docs/product/scenarios.md` — `### Scenario N` headings define
  the scenario IDs (extracted as `SCN-1`, `SCN-2`, … `SCN-20`,
  with the unusual ordering preserved)
- `docs/architecture/decision-log.md` — `### D0NN ·` and
  `# D0NN —` headings define ADR IDs
- `docs/build/bu-sequence.md` and
  `docs/build/session-briefs/*.md` — Build Unit names referenced
  in `**Build Unit:**` lines and as `BU-<name>` in prose
- All `.ts` / `.tsx` / `.js` files under `app/`, `components/`,
  `server/`, `shared/`, `prisma/` — `@build-unit`, `@spec`,
  `@scenarios` tags in JSDoc headers
- `eslint-rules/canonical-areas.json` — the area-prefix list,
  used to cross-reference testid coverage per area

### Outputs produced

Three modes:

**`npm run trace <id>` (default — single-ID lookup)**

```
$ npm run trace SCN-20
SCN-20 — Eddie writes his first comment

  Build Units (1):
    BU-comments

  ADRs (3):
    D045 — post visibility
    D050 — Reaction polymorphic schema (parent)
    D052 — Comment schema

  Code files (8):
    app/post/[id]/actions.ts
    app/post/[id]/page.tsx
    components/CommentComposer.tsx
    components/CommentItem.tsx
    components/CommentList.tsx
    server/routers/comment.ts
    server/services/comment.ts
    shared/validation/comment.ts

  Tests (2):
    tests/integration/comment-service.test.ts
    tests/unit/comment-router.test.ts

  Testid area prefixes (1):
    comment

  Coverage: ✓ scenario has 8 backing code files
```

Resolves any of: `SCN-N`, `BU-<name>`, `D0NN`, file path, testid
area prefix.

**`npm run trace:check` (CI mode)**

Exits non-zero if any of:

- A scenario has zero referencing code files
- A code file has `@spec product/scenarios.md (SCN-N)` referencing
  an SCN that doesn't exist in scenarios.md
- A code file has `@spec architecture/decision-log.md (D0NN)`
  referencing a D-number that doesn't exist
- A `@build-unit BU-<name>` references a name not in the canonical
  list (per D051)

Output: a list of failing references with file:line.

Threshold tuning: scenarios that are explicitly parked (no code
expected yet) carry an `<!-- @no-code-yet -->` marker in
scenarios.md that the check honours. See open question 3.

**`npm run trace:matrix` (regenerate the markdown)**

Writes `docs/architecture/traceability-matrix.md`:

```
# Traceability matrix

Generated by `scripts/trace.ts` on 2026-04-26.

## Scenarios

| SCN  | Title                            | BUs           | ADRs          | Files | Tests |
| ---- | -------------------------------- | ------------- | ------------- | ----- | ----- |
| SCN-1| Sharon sees a Sky News bias post | BU-share-out, BU-feed | D045 | 0 | 0 |
| SCN-3| David reacts to Shabbat post     | BU-reactions  | D050          | 8     | 2     |
| SCN-18| Eddie writes his first post     | BU-composer   | D045, D048    | 6     | 4     |
| SCN-20| Eddie writes his first comment  | BU-comments   | D045, D050, D052 | 8 | 2 |
| ...  | ...                              | ...           | ...           | ...   | ...   |

## Coverage gaps

- SCN-1: 0 code files (Sharon's WhatsApp share is a future BU)
- SCN-2: 0 code files (Quick Snap composer = BU-composer-fab)
- ...

## Build Units

| BU            | Status   | SCNs         | ADRs       | Files |
| ------------- | -------- | ------------ | ---------- | ----- |
| BU-001-lite   | shipped  | (none)       | -          | 11    |
| BU-feed       | shipped  | SCN-18       | D045       | 4     |
| BU-composer   | shipped  | SCN-18       | D045, D048 | 6     |
| BU-reactions  | shipped  | SCN-3        | D050       | 8     |
| BU-comments   | shipped  | SCN-20       | D050, D052 | 8     |
| BU-auth       | not started | SCN-5     | -          | 0     |
| ...           | ...      | ...          | ...        | ...   |
```

The matrix is committed; `trace:check` also verifies it's not
drifted (i.e. that running `trace:matrix` produces the same file).

---

## Acceptance criteria

### Functional

- [ ] D053 ADR appended (or skipped — see open questions)
- [ ] `scripts/trace.ts` exists; runs via `tsx`
- [ ] `npm run trace SCN-20` prints the forward + reverse tree
      with file:line precision
- [ ] `npm run trace BU-comments` prints the same tree centred on
      a Build Unit
- [ ] `npm run trace D052` prints same centred on an ADR
- [ ] `npm run trace components/CommentList.tsx` prints the file's
      backing scenarios + BUs + ADRs
- [ ] `npm run trace:check` exits 0 when all references resolve
      and every scenario has ≥1 backing code file (or carries
      `<!-- @no-code-yet -->`)
- [ ] `npm run trace:check` exits 1 with a precise file:line list
      when references break
- [ ] `npm run trace:matrix` regenerates
      `docs/architecture/traceability-matrix.md` deterministically
- [ ] `traceability-matrix.md` is committed; CI fails if it's
      drifted (running matrix produces a different file)
- [ ] CI workflow has a "Traceability check" step before tests
- [ ] `docs/process/traceability.md` documents the conventions

### Non-functional

- [ ] TypeScript compiles with zero errors, zero `any`, zero
      `@ts-ignore`
- [ ] Script files carry `@build-unit BU-trace` + `@spec
      process/traceability.md` headers (eats own dog food)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] New tests pass (6+ unit cases for parsing, cross-ref, and
      check-mode logic)
- [ ] Lint passes — F06 rule 1 + F13 + F14 untouched
- [ ] Prettier clean
- [ ] `npm run trace SCN-20` completes in <2s on a cold cache
- [ ] Commit message: `chore(tooling): BU-trace —
      scripts/trace.ts + traceability matrix`

### What "ready to merge" looks like

- All acceptance criteria pass
- Generated `traceability-matrix.md` committed and reviewed for
  surprises (any unexpected gaps?)
- The "Coverage gaps" section in the matrix is reviewed — we
  expect gaps for parked-scenario BUs (SCN-1, SCN-2, etc.); the
  brief author should ack each gap explicitly
- README explains how to run the three modes

---

## Tests required

**Unit (parsing):**

1. Extract `@build-unit BU-name` from a JSDoc header — handle
   single-line, multi-line, with `'use client'` directive above
2. Extract `@spec product/scenarios.md (SCN-N)` references —
   handle multiple `@spec` tags, with and without parens, with
   trailing whitespace
3. Extract `@spec architecture/decision-log.md (D0NN)` — same
4. Extract scenario IDs from `docs/product/scenarios.md` — handle
   the unusual SCN ordering (`### Scenario 18` appears between 6
   and 7)
5. Extract ADR IDs from `decision-log.md` — handle both `### D0NN ·`
   and `# D0NN —` heading styles
6. Cross-reference: given a scenario, return the list of files
   that `@spec` it
7. Cross-reference: given a file, return the scenarios it `@spec`s
8. Honour `<!-- @no-code-yet -->` marker in scenarios.md

**Unit (check mode):**

9. `--check` exits 0 when all references resolve
10. `--check` exits 1 when a code file references a non-existent
    SCN or D-number
11. `--check` exits 1 when a scenario has zero references and no
    `@no-code-yet` marker

**Integration:**

12. `--matrix` against a tiny fixture (3 scenarios, 2 files)
    produces a deterministic markdown file
13. Running matrix twice in a row produces the same output (no
    timestamps drifting; date is captured but the rest is stable)

**Manual:**

14. Run `npm run trace SCN-20` against the real codebase; verify
    output makes sense
15. Run `npm run trace:check` against main; expect zero exits
    cleanly (or the failures are real gaps to address)

**Not required:**

- Performance benchmarks beyond the <2s soft target
- Full-fidelity Markdown round-trip parsing (regex is enough)

---

## Scenarios to verify against

This BU has **no user-facing scenario**. It's tooling, not a
feature. Per the audit raised earlier in this work stream,
infrastructure / Phase 0 / dev-tooling BUs intentionally lack
scenarios — `ratchet-discipline.md` is the spec.

The script's *output* is verified against existing scenarios,
which gives a useful smoke test:

- SCN-3, SCN-18, SCN-20: should report ≥1 backing code file each
  (these are shipped scenarios)
- SCN-1, SCN-2, SCN-4–17, SCN-19: expected to report 0 backing
  files (parked scenarios; the matrix surfaces them as expected
  gaps)

---

## Known gotchas

- **Mixed ADR heading styles.** Some ADRs use `### D0NN ·` and
  some use `# D0NN —`. The parser must handle both. (See decision-
  log lines 1238 and 1553 for `# D0NN —` examples.)
- **D-number monotonicity.** ADR numbers are not always sequential
  (D047 was used for a different decision than what was originally
  proposed; the brief that drafted D050 picked it because D047 was
  taken). The parser shouldn't assume gaps are bugs — it just
  catalogues what exists.
- **`@spec` value normalisation.** A file might have `@spec
  product/scenarios.md (SCN-3)` or `@spec
  product/scenarios.md SCN-3` or both forms across files. The
  parser normalises to a canonical `(spec-path, ref)` tuple.
- **`@scenarios` tag (legacy).** Some early files use `@scenarios
  SCN-04` instead of `@spec product/scenarios.md (SCN-4)`. Per
  D038 the canonical form is `@spec`; the parser accepts both
  formats but prints a warning when it sees the legacy form,
  letting cleanup happen incrementally.
- **Build Unit name resolution.** Per D051, BU names are
  semantic. The parser reads
  `eslint-rules/canonical-areas.json`-style canonical sources or
  bu-sequence.md to resolve known BU names. Unknown BU names
  surface as warnings (not failures) for forward-compat with new
  BUs.
- **Zero-files-but-parked scenarios.** Default behaviour: fail.
  Override: `<!-- @no-code-yet -->` HTML comment on the line
  immediately after the scenario heading. Document in
  scenarios.md adoption notes.
- **`scripts/seed.ts` is excluded** from `@build-unit` enforcement
  per the existing F06 ignore globs. The trace script honours the
  same excludes — seeding code is "infrastructure" and doesn't
  need scenario backing.

## Definition of done

- [ ] `scripts/trace.ts` exists, runs, passes its tests
- [ ] `npm run trace`, `trace:check`, `trace:matrix` all wired in
      package.json
- [ ] `docs/process/traceability.md` written
- [ ] `docs/architecture/traceability-matrix.md` generated and
      committed
- [ ] CI step added (`Traceability check`)
- [ ] D053 added (or, if open question 1 says skip, justified
      in the PR description)
- [ ] `npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test` all green
- [ ] `npm run trace:check` exits 0 against current main
- [ ] Manual run of `npm run trace SCN-20` produces sensible output
- [ ] PR description: links this brief, screenshots the trace
      output for SCN-20, summarises what gaps the matrix surfaced
- [ ] Commit message: `chore(tooling): BU-trace —
      scripts/trace.ts + traceability matrix`
- [ ] Branch: `phase-0/bu-trace`

---

## Open questions to surface

Things this session cannot decide autonomously. Surface; do not
assume.

1. **D053 ADR — needed or not?** D038 already specs the discipline
   and the script. D053 would add output-format and check-threshold
   decisions. Recommend: **add D053** for the threshold and
   parked-scenario marker decisions; they're the kind of thing
   that benefits from a recorded rationale.

2. **`@no-code-yet` marker syntax.** HTML comment vs frontmatter
   vs an inline shortcode. Recommend: HTML comment
   (`<!-- @no-code-yet -->`) since scenarios.md is plain markdown
   and the comment is invisible in rendered view.

3. **Threshold for scenarios with zero refs.** Today many
   scenarios (1, 2, 4–17, 19) have no code. Brief recommends
   ship-with-honest-gaps — the matrix shows them, the check
   tolerates them via `@no-code-yet` markers. Confirm: do we mark
   all 17 parked scenarios up-front in this PR, or let the next
   PR opening BU-X do it for the relevant scenarios?

4. **Matrix file path.** `docs/architecture/traceability-matrix.md`
   per the brief. Alternative: `docs/build/traceability-matrix.md`
   (build-flavoured artefact). Recommend: architecture (it's
   structural, like ADRs).

5. **Drift check on the matrix.** Brief recommends CI fails when
   running `trace:matrix` produces a different file from the
   committed one. Alternative: don't commit the matrix at all and
   regenerate on demand. Recommend: commit + drift-check (gives
   reviewers a one-glance snapshot in PRs).

6. **Performance soft target (<2s on cold cache).** Generous for
   a small repo; may need adjustment as repo grows. Confirm.

7. **CI step placement.** Recommend after lint, before tests
   (fast-fail on traceability before slow test suite).

8. **Should the check fail on legacy `@scenarios` tags** or just
   warn? Recommend: warn (forward-compat for in-flight cleanup).
   Hard error after a deliberate cleanup pass.

9. **Cross-reference depth.** Brief outputs direct references only
   (file → scenario, scenario → file). Transitive chains
   (scenario → BU → ADR → other BUs that depend on it) are
   future. Confirm.

10. **Adoption rollout.** Once shipped, every new code file
    needing scenario backing gets `@spec product/scenarios.md
    (SCN-N)` per D038. Existing files without SCN refs are not
    retroactively tagged in this PR — only by future BUs as they
    touch the file. Confirm.

(Claude Code: add any further judgement calls encountered during
implementation.)

## Context

**Specs (read first):**

- `docs/architecture/decision-log.md` — D038 (the parent ADR;
  this BU implements §6); D051 (BU naming); D052 (recent ADR
  numbering pattern reference)
- `docs/process/ratchet-discipline.md` — same philosophical
  spirit (mechanically enforced, forward-only)
- `docs/process/api-contract-discipline.md` — sibling discipline
  doc to traceability.md
- `docs/process/testid-convention.md` — F14's convention; the
  trace script reads `canonical-areas.json` to surface testid
  coverage

**Existing code to read first:**

- `eslint-rules/rules/require-build-unit-header.js` and
  `eslint-rules/rules/require-spec-tag.js` — both parse the same
  header tags the trace script reads. Reuse the regex and parser
  conventions.
- `eslint-rules/canonical-areas.json` — input
- Any 2-3 typical feature files (e.g. `server/services/comment.ts`,
  `components/CommentList.tsx`, `app/post/[id]/page.tsx`) — to
  see the typical header shape

**Process:**

- `docs/process/session-brief-template.md` — this brief follows it
- `docs/process/session-hygiene.md` — context discipline

---

## What this brief does NOT cover

(Naming gaps explicitly.)

1. **Auto-fix / auto-tagging.** No insertion of `@spec` tags by
   the script.
2. **IDE integration.** No language-server, no VS Code extension.
3. **Watch mode.** Run on demand or in CI; no file-watching.
4. **PR-time diff posting.** No "this PR changes the matrix as
   follows" comment-bot.
5. **Visual graph rendering.** Markdown only.
6. **Cross-repo traceability.** Single repo.
7. **Analytics-event coverage.** Out for now; can extend later.
8. **Testid-level reverse lookup.** Area prefixes are enough for
   MVP.
9. **Author / blame info.** `git log` covers it.

---

## Slice convention

BU-trace is a **chore(tooling)** session — adds a script + docs +
CI step, no feature code. Establishes:

- The materialised traceability matrix as a recurring artefact
- The `@no-code-yet` marker convention for parked scenarios
- The trace.ts script as the entry point for any "is this
  scenario covered?" / "what does this file serve?" question

Future enhancements parking-lot:
- Mermaid / Graphviz output
- Watch mode
- IDE integration
- Cross-event coverage

---

## What lands after this session

- One command answers any traceability question the codebase can
  resolve
- CI surfaces broken `@spec` references at PR time, not at
  archaeology time
- `docs/architecture/traceability-matrix.md` is a one-glance
  health snapshot; reviewers see at a glance which scenarios are
  shipped vs parked
- D038 §6 commitment is fulfilled (Phase 0 deliverable shipped,
  ~2 months later than originally planned but delivered)
- The next BU's brief automatically inherits trace coverage
  expectations — when BU-auth's brief gets written, the brief
  author will check the matrix and see "SCN-5 currently has 0
  refs; this BU should change that"

Next BU candidates: pick from the existing demo-leverage roadmap
(BU-auth for pilot path, BU-attachments for slice 2 closure,
BU-composer-fab for richer UX). BU-trace doesn't gate any of them.
